name: AZQ Stage 2 Wave D Checks
purpose: Define task-specific automated checks for Stage 2 Wave D so the Codex runner can evaluate the Stage 2 refactor-prep work without hardcoding Wave D architecture logic into the runner.

baseline_checks.name: compileall
baseline_checks.description: Verify the azq package still compiles after the refactor patch.

baseline_checks.name: stage2_regression_suite
baseline_checks.description: Verify the existing Stage 2 regression suite still passes while Wave D refactors preserve behavior.

baseline_checks.name: formam_modules_import
baseline_checks.description: Verify the live Formam command and storage modules still import cleanly.

baseline_checks.name: ritual_shell_syntax
baseline_checks.description: Verify the ritual shell wrapper still parses cleanly.

STAGE2-WAVED-01.description: Extract Formam path and schema primitives into focused modules.

checks.name: formam_path_and_schema_modules_exist
checks.description: Verify the new focused Formam paths and schemas modules exist.

checks.name: formam_path_and_schema_modules_import_cleanly
checks.description: Verify the extracted Formam path and schema modules import cleanly and storage still exposes the public Stage 2 helpers.

human_checks: azq/formam/storage.py is visibly narrower after the extraction and no longer reads like the only home for every Formam concern.
The new module names feel intuitive to an operator or contributor reading the tree for the first time.
No new craft-layer behavior was introduced while extracting paths and schemas.

STAGE2-WAVED-02.description: Extract deliverable repository and markdown helpers from Formam storage.

checks.name: deliverable_storage_module_exists
checks.description: Verify the dedicated deliverable storage module exists.

checks.name: deliverable_storage_module_exposes_core_helpers
checks.description: Verify the extracted deliverable storage module exposes the core Stage 2 deliverable helpers.
checks.code:
from azq.formam import deliverable_storage
for name in ['serialize_deliverable_record', 'parse_deliverable_record', 'load_all_deliverables', 'load_deliverable', 'next_deliverable_id', 'write_deliverable']:
    assert hasattr(deliverable_storage, name), name
assert hasattr(deliverable_storage, 'load_goal_deliverables') or hasattr(deliverable_storage, 'load_deliverables_for_goal')

checks.name: build_and_deliverables_reference_extracted_deliverable_module
checks.command:
python - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
for path in ['azq/formam/build.py', 'azq/formam/deliverables.py']:
    text = Path(path).read_text(encoding='utf-8')
    assert 'deliverable_storage' in text, path
PY
checks.description: Verify the command-layer modules reference the extracted deliverable storage module instead of relying only on the old grab-bag storage file.

human_checks: build.py and deliverables.py now depend on a clearer deliverable-specific boundary rather than reaching into a catch-all storage module.
The extracted module preserves the canonical DELIV_### markdown format and deterministic repository behavior.

STAGE2-WAVED-03.description: Extract goal-map repository helpers and move dependency-edge derivation beside map logic.

checks.name: map_storage_module_exists
checks.description: Verify the dedicated goal-map storage module exists.

checks.name: map_storage_module_exposes_core_helpers
checks.description: Verify the extracted goal-map storage module exposes the core Stage 2 map helpers.

checks.name: dependency_edge_derivation_moved_out_of_storage_module_body
checks.description: Verify dependency-edge derivation now lives in a map-focused module rather than being defined directly inside azq.formam.storage.

human_checks: Goal-map shaping code is now easier to find than it was in the all-in-one storage module.
The refactor preserves the sparse, human-readable Stage 2 map behavior and does not drift into Agenda-style graph planning.

STAGE2-WAVED-04.description: Split azq/cli.py into thinner engine-specific command routers.

checks.name: engine_specific_cli_modules_exist
checks.command: test -f azq/scintilla/cli.py && test -f azq/finis/cli.py && test -f azq/formam/cli.py
checks.description: Verify the new engine-specific CLI router modules exist.

checks.name: engine_specific_cli_modules_import_cleanly
checks.description: Verify the top-level CLI and extracted engine-specific router modules import cleanly.

checks.name: top_level_cli_is_thinner_and_routes_to_engine_modules
checks.command:
python - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
text = Path('azq/cli.py').read_text(encoding='utf-8')
line_count = len(text.splitlines())
assert line_count <= 200, line_count
for snippet in ['azq.scintilla.cli', 'azq.finis.cli', 'azq.formam.cli']:
    assert snippet in text, snippet
assert 'def main' in text
PY
checks.description: Verify azq/cli.py has been reduced to a thinner top-level entrypoint that routes to engine-specific CLI modules.

human_checks: The Finis readiness trigger moved to a narrower place without breaking the existing Formam command loop.
The split kept public Stage 2 command behavior stable instead of adding new commands or new craft-layer logic.

STAGE2-WAVED-05.description: Rename the staged task runner to match its real scope and keep a thin compatibility path.

checks.name: stage_agnostic_runner_exists
checks.description: Verify the new stage-agnostic runner entrypoint exists.

checks.name: old_and_new_runner_entrypoints_both_report_cleanly
checks.command: python azq_codex_task_runner.py report --workspace . >/dev/null && python azq_codex_stage1_task_runner.py report --workspace . >/dev/null
checks.description: Verify the renamed runner works and the old Stage-1-named path still acts as a compatibility entrypoint.

checks.name: ritual_and_runbooks_reference_stage_agnostic_runner
checks.command:
python - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
for path in ['run_next_task_in_current_wave.sh', 'AZQ_CODEX_RUNBOOK_STAGE_1_WAVE_A.md', 'AZQ_CODEX_RUNBOOK_STAGE_2_WAVE_A.md']:
    text = Path(path).read_text(encoding='utf-8')
    assert 'azq_codex_task_runner.py' in text, path
PY
checks.description: Verify the shell ritual and live runbooks now reference the stage-agnostic runner name.

human_checks: The compatibility path stays thin rather than duplicating full runner logic under both filenames.
The rename improves the operator-facing language without redesigning staged task semantics.

STAGE2-WAVED-06.description: Add regression coverage for the Wave D refactor seams.

checks.name: stage2_regression_suite_still_passes_after_wave_d
checks.description: Verify the Stage 2 regression suite passes after the Wave D refactors and test updates.

checks.name: stage2_tests_reference_wave_d_refactor_surfaces
checks.command:
python - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
text = Path('tests/test_stage2_wave_c.py').read_text(encoding='utf-8')
matched = [
    snippet for snippet in [
        'azq.formam.paths',
        'azq.formam.schemas',
        'azq.formam.deliverable_storage',
        'azq.formam.map_storage',
        'azq.formam.cli',
        'azq_codex_task_runner.py'
    ]
    if snippet in text
]
assert len(matched) >= 4, matched
PY
checks.description: Verify the Stage 2 regression file was extended to cover the major public seams introduced by Wave D.

human_checks: The new assertions focus on preserved public behavior and invokable entrypoints rather than overfitting to one exact internal import graph.
The added coverage remains readable and does not turn tests/test_stage2_wave_c.py into an unmaintainable architecture snapshot.
